comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## S6 implemented + APPLY-PROVE-ROLLBACK on the live navapbc/rebar repo — 2026-06-30 (commit c4a8be68)

Per the chosen scope (apply, prove, then roll back) — the live repo is back to its ORIGINAL state; no permanent change.

**IaC (infra/terraform-github/):** two `github_repository_ruleset` (branch `main`: rules update+deletion+non_fast_forward; tags `refs/tags/**`: creation+update+deletion), DeployKey-only bypass (`actor_type=DeployKey, bypass_mode=always`, NO actor_id — sending null is API-rejected), provider `integrations/github >=6.8.0` (resolved 6.12.1), separate state key `rebar/prod/github.tfstate`, a `check` existence-gate over the deploy keys (fails loudly if S5's key absent). The `update` rule is the lock — it rejects direct pushes AND PR-merges for non-bypass actors.

**Live apply-prove-rollback (proof):**
1. terraform apply -> created gerrit-mirror-lock-main (18306896) + gerrit-mirror-lock-tags (18306898), both active.
2. Deleted the pre-existing main-protection (18048287) -> lock is the sole governor.
3. PROVED every non-bypass operation REJECTED (I am repo admin = non-bypass; only the DeployKey bypasses):
   - direct `git push` to main -> `GH013 ... Cannot update this protected ref` (REJECTED).
   - force-push to main -> REJECTED (GH013).
   - PR-merge to main (PR #99, `gh pr merge --admin`) -> REJECTED (`Repository rule violations ... Cannot update this protected ref`).
   - tag push -> REJECTED by the tag ruleset (GH013 for refs/tags/).
   - main deletion -> REJECTED.
   main HEAD unchanged by me (every proof push declined).
4. ROLLED BACK: terraform destroy (both lock rulesets removed) + recreated main-protection from infra/github/main-protection.snapshot.json. Closed/deleted the proof PR + branch.
5. VERIFIED final state == original: only `main-protection` active; its rules match the snapshot EXACTLY (deletion, non_fast_forward, pull_request, required_status_checks); no stray lock rulesets or proof tags on the remote.

**AC mapping:** direct-push/PR-merge/force-push/deletion REJECTED (AC1) ✓; tag push REJECTED (AC2) ✓; native DeployKey bypass, sole bypass, idempotent (AC3) ✓; created active in a single terraform apply, no null_resource/gh-api-bypass/two-phase (AC4) ✓; deploy key obtained via data-source existence gate (AC5) ✓; main-protection replaced during the test, restored on rollback (AC6) ✓. Runbook: infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md (cutover + <15min rollback). No secrets committed (token env/CI-sourced).

**Code review (subagent):** VERDICT PASS — terraform correct, DeployKey bypass form correct, rollback whitelists create-time fields (no repo-destroy risk), no secrets, no perpetual diff. 2 NITs on the NON-default `--disable` rollback path (the proven default delete+recreate path needs no change).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLOSE: close gate(s) bypassed by user approval — no completion/signature attestation was signed. Reason: "Operator chose apply-prove-rollback scope (confirmed via question): the deploy-key-only lock was APPLIED + EMPIRICALLY PROVEN on the live navapbc/rebar (human push, force-push, PR-merge incl --admin, tag push, deletion all REJECTED), then ROLLED BACK to restore main-protection (verified == snapshot). The permanent-cutover ACs (live PRs/Issues/Actions disabled, README/About mirror banner, root CONTRIBUTING.md replacing PR guidance, GitHub-PR guidance removed) are DELIBERATELY NOT applied — they would misrepresent the rolled-back repo and change the team's live workflow, which the operator did not opt into. The full cutover playbook (feature toggles, banner text, Gerrit-only contributor workflow) is committed in infra/runbooks/github-mirror-lock.md for the eventual real cutover. Lock mechanism + rollback are proven; closed-without-signature reflects the intentionally-deferred permanent-cutover steps.". Session: d6f23ff8.

compiled_state.description:
# S6 — Apply the GitHub mirror lock (repo-level ruleset, deploy-key-only push)

## Why
Enforce that GitHub `main` can ONLY be advanced by the Gerrit replication deploy key. The lock
is a REPO-LEVEL ruleset (navapbc is a free org -> admin-proof ORG-level rulesets need a paid
plan; trusted-admin editability is the accepted risk, recorded in S6-pre's ADR). Applies the
lock that S6-pre prepared (snapshot + ADR + backend/token). Proven empirically (derisk): an
active repo-level ruleset with deploy-key-only bypass REJECTS a repo-admin's direct push
(`GH013`), so "no human/admin bypass" holds.

## What
- **Use the native provider (peer-pattern simplification).** `terraform-provider-github`
  **v6.8.0 (Nov 2025, PR #2726)** added native `DeployKey` bypass support (removed the
  actor_type whitelist; made `actor_id` optional). So the whole prior `null_resource` + `gh api`
  + `ignore_changes` + disabled->active two-phase dance is **dropped** — it existed only because
  the provider rejected `DeployKey`. Pin `integrations/github >= 6.8.0`.
- One `github_repository_ruleset` for the branch lock on `main` (+ any other branch the bot
  pushes), created directly at `enforcement = "active"` in a single apply, with rules
  `update` + `creation` + `deletion` + `non_fast_forward` and a single
  `bypass_actors { actor_type = "DeployKey"  bypass_mode = "always" }` (NO `actor_id` — it's
  optional; GitHub normalizes to `null`, which the empirical spike confirmed is canonical and
  accepted). A SEPARATE `github_repository_ruleset` for tags (`refs/tags/**`, target `tag`) with
  the same rules + the same DeployKey bypass (else tag pushes are rejected).
- **Replace the existing `main-protection` ruleset** (DERISK E5: id 18048287, PR +
  required-status-checks model — incompatible with deploy-key-only push). It is snapshotted in
  S6-pre; S6 deletes it (`gh api -X DELETE` or via the provider after `terraform import`) and
  creates the mirror-lock rulesets. There is no PR/status-check ruleset left once S6 applies.
- Consume S5's deploy key via a `data "github_repository_deploy_key"` (or a remote-state ref to
  S5's `deploy_key_id` output) so the dependency is explicit and S6 cannot apply before S5's key
  exists. (The bypass entry itself carries no actor_id; the data-source lookup is the existence
  gate, failing the apply loudly if the key is absent.)
- **Token scope.** The Terraform identity needs `Administration: write` on `navapbc/rebar`
  (fine-grained PAT scoped to this repo, or classic `repo`), injected at apply time as a
  `sensitive` var from a CI secret, never committed to `.tfvars`.
- **Remote state.** `infra/terraform/github.tf` uses the shared remote backend with state
  locking; `terraform plan` is reviewed before every `apply`.
- Mirror hygiene: turn OFF Pull Requests (Feb-2026 repo setting), disable Issues + Actions; add
  a "mirror — contribute via Gerrit at rebar.solutions.navateam.com" banner to the About +
  README; do NOT archive (archiving freezes the replication bot too).
- Rollback (runnable in ~15 min): set the rulesets `enforcement = disabled` (provider apply, or
  `gh api PATCH .../rulesets/{id} -f enforcement=disabled` for speed), restore `main-protection`
  from S6-pre's snapshot, and re-enable PRs/Issues/Actions.

## Scope
In: the two native ruleset resources (branch + tag) with DeployKey bypass, replacing
main-protection, mirror hygiene, rollback. Out: the snapshot/ADR/token-scope pre-work (S6-pre);
the Gerrit-side replication (S5); the end-to-end push-past-the-ruleset proof (S7).
**Dependency:** apply AFTER S6-pre (snapshot + ADR + backend ready) AND S5 (deploy key exists).

## Files
- `infra/terraform/github.tf` — provider pin (`>= 6.8.0`), remote backend, sensitive token var,
  the two `github_repository_ruleset` resources (branch + tag, `enforcement = active`, DeployKey
  bypass), the `data.github_repository_deploy_key` lookup, and the repo-feature toggles
  (PRs/Issues/Actions off, mirror banner).
- (optional) a small documented step to delete the pre-existing `main-protection` ruleset by id
  (or `terraform import` then replace), referencing S6-pre's snapshot.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A direct `git push` / PR-merge to `main` by a non-bypass identity is REJECTED; force-push
      and deletion of `main` are REJECTED. (Empirically confirmed for the admin-push case.)
- [ ] A tag push by a non-bypass identity is REJECTED by the separate `refs/tags/**` ruleset.
- [ ] The DeployKey bypass is expressed natively as `bypass_actors { actor_type = "DeployKey"
      bypass_mode = "always" }` (no `actor_id`) via `terraform-provider-github >= 6.8.0`, and is
      the ONLY bypass entry on each ruleset; re-running `terraform apply` is idempotent with no
      perpetual diff (provider stores `actor_id = null`, matching GitHub's normalization).
- [ ] The rulesets are created at `enforcement = "active"` in a single apply (NO null_resource,
      NO gh-api bypass step, NO disabled->active two-phase dance, NO `ignore_changes`).
- [ ] S6 obtains the deploy key via a data source / remote-state ref (not a hand-copied ID);
      applying before S5's key exists fails loudly.
- [ ] The pre-existing `main-protection` ruleset (PR + required-status-checks) is replaced/removed
      (snapshot kept in S6-pre); no PR/status-check ruleset remains on `main`.
- [ ] PRs disabled, Issues disabled, Actions disabled; README/About carry the mirror banner;
      repo NOT archived.
- [ ] The Terraform identity's `Administration: write` scope is documented and injected as a
      `sensitive` var from a CI secret (never in `.tfvars`); `github.tf` uses the shared remote
      backend with locking.
- [ ] A rollback runnable in ~15 min is documented (set `enforcement=disabled`, restore
      `main-protection` from the S6-pre snapshot, re-enable PRs/Issues/Actions).

## Plan-review remediation (Round-12, 2026-06-29)
- The Gerrit-only workflow makes the existing GitHub-PR contributor docs stale/misleading. Add contributor-facing docs as part of the mirror cutover.

Added ACs:
- [ ] CONTRIBUTING.md + README updated for the Gerrit-only workflow (push `HEAD:refs/for/main`, git-review setup, SSH key registration on Gerrit, the LLM-Review gate) so GitHub-PR guidance is no longer presented; the GitHub mirror banner links to it

file_impact.reason: branch+tag rulesets, DeployKey bypass, deploy-key data source, token var
restore main-protection from snapshot + disable lock

compiled_state.title: infra: S6 — Lock GitHub repo to read-only mirror (repo-level ruleset)
